
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║        🔒 SEALED & LOCKED MODULE PROMPT — ECOSKILLER PLATFORM                              ║
║        MODULE ID         :  VIDEO_CONNECT                                                   ║
║        MODULE CODENAME   :  ANTIGRAVITY — MEDIA LAYER                                       ║
║        MODULE TYPE       :  REAL-TIME VIDEO / VOICE / MEDIA ENGINE                          ║
║        VERSION           :  1.0.0-LOCKED                                                    ║
║        PARENT SYSTEM     :  ECOSKILLER ENTERPRISE MULTI-TENANT SAAS                        ║
║        PARENT MODULE     :  ANALYTICS_CONNECT (ANTIGRAVITY)                                 ║
║        STAGE             :  STAGE 2 — INTELLIGENCE (SEQUENTIAL DEPENDENCY ENFORCED)        ║
║        MUTATION POLICY   :  ADD_ONLY                                                        ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🔐 EXECUTION POLICY (HARD LOCK — ABSOLUTE — NO EXCEPTIONS)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

EXECUTION_MODE                = LOCKED
MUTATION_POLICY               = ADD_ONLY
CREATIVE_INTERPRETATION       = FORBIDDEN
ASSUMPTION_FILLING            = FORBIDDEN
DEFAULT_BEHAVIOR              = DENY
FAILURE_MODE                  = STOP_EXECUTION
OVERRIDE_AUTHORITY            = PLATFORM_ADMIN_ONLY
DUPLICATION_POLICY            = FORBIDDEN (inherit, do not redeclare)
CONFLICT_POLICY               = DENY
CROSS_TENANT_ACCESS           = FORBIDDEN → SECURITY_FAILURE
CROSS_DOMAIN_LEAK             = FORBIDDEN → SECURITY_FAILURE
AI_APPROVAL_AUTHORITY         = NONE (AI ADVISES ONLY — NEVER ACTS)
HARDCODED_ROLES               = FORBIDDEN
BACKEND_IN_FRONTEND           = FORBIDDEN
MEDIA_THROUGH_API             = FORBIDDEN (media plane strictly separate)
TRANSPORT_MODEL_CHANGE        = REQUIRES VERSION BUMP (not a patch)
PROVIDER_SWAP                 = ALLOWED (architecture is not swappable)
REACT_IN_LIVE_ROOM            = FORBIDDEN
RECORDING_WITHOUT_CONSENT     = FORBIDDEN → BLOCK EXECUTION
UNSIGNED_MEDIA_TOKEN          = FORBIDDEN → SECURITY_FAILURE
ANONYMOUS_PARTICIPANT         = FORBIDDEN
SKIP_STAGE_POLICY             = INVALID BUILD → STOP EXECUTION

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1️⃣  MODULE IDENTITY (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

MODULE_CODENAME               = ANTIGRAVITY — MEDIA LAYER
MODULE_CLASS                  = VIDEO_CONNECT
MODULE_ROLE                   = Platform-wide real-time voice, video, recording,
                                proctoring, and media infrastructure engine
MODULE_SCOPE                  = All real-time sessions: Dojo GD rooms,
                                Interview rooms, Trainer live classrooms,
                                Mock interview rooms, Mentor control sessions,
                                Arena live sessions
MODULE_ISOLATION              = Hard per-tenant room namespacing
                                + per-session signed token isolation
MODULE_ACCESS                 = RBAC + ABAC inherited from parent
STAGE_DEPENDENCY              = STAGE 1 (Foundation) complete
                                + Auth Service, Session Service operational
                                + Consent Management Service operational

PURPOSE:
  VIDEO_CONNECT is the isolated, hardened real-time media engine of the
  Ecoskiller platform. It governs all voice, video, recording, screen-share,
  proctoring, and quality-of-service operations across every session type.

  VIDEO_CONNECT:
    ─ Issues short-lived, signed media tokens (never raw credentials)
    ─ Manages room lifecycle (creation → join → session → teardown)
    ─ Enforces turn-based speaking, forced mute/unmute (Dojo GD)
    ─ Captures recording only after explicit, per-session consent
    ─ Monitors real-time QoS (audio/video latency, jitter, packet loss)
    ─ Reconnects participants gracefully within 3 seconds of drop
    ─ Generates immutable audit logs for every media event
    ─ Feeds session signals to ANALYTICS_CONNECT via Kafka events

WHAT VIDEO_CONNECT IS NOT:
    ─ NOT a general-purpose CDN or file server (MinIO handles storage)
    ─ NOT a signaling proxy for business logic (business logic stays in services)
    ─ NOT accessible from React web layer (Flutter app only for live rooms)
    ─ NOT a recording store (recordings stored in MinIO / object store)
    ─ NOT a decision-making system (all outputs are advisory or enforced
      only within the media plane per pre-configured session rules)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
2️⃣  INHERITANCE CONTRACT (DO NOT RE-DECLARE — REFERENCE ONLY)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

VIDEO_CONNECT inherits and enforces (already finalized in parent):

  ✅ Authorization              → RBAC + ABAC at token issuance boundary
  ✅ Authentication             → JWT + OAuth2 + MFA (no room join without auth)
  ✅ Tenant Isolation           → Hard: rooms namespaced by tenant_id
  ✅ Domain Track Isolation     → Arts | Commerce | Science | Technology | Admin
  ✅ Consent Management (CM-*)  → Explicit per-session consent before recording
                                  (CM-A through CM-O fully enforced)
  ✅ Audit Logging (AL-*)       → All media events immutably logged
  ✅ Data Access Logging (DAL-*)→ Recording access, replay access logged
  ✅ PII Masking (PM-*)         → No PII overlay in recordings; transcripts redacted
  ✅ Rate Limiting (RL-*)       → Message rate, join/leave, recording access throttled
  ✅ Data Integrity (DI-*)      → Recording metadata immutable; score locked
  ✅ Backup & Restore (BR-*)    → Recording backups; media opt-in only
  ✅ Encryption at Rest         → AES-256 (all recording files)
  ✅ Encryption in Transit      → TLS 1.3 (signaling), SRTP (media plane)
  ✅ AI Advisory Policy         → AI advises only (QoS, anti-cheat scores);
                                  never blocks participants without human command

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
3️⃣  MEDIA STACK ARCHITECTURE (HARD LOCKED — ALL COMPONENTS REQUIRED)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

TRAFFIC CLASS ISOLATION (LOCKED — NO MIXING):
  CLASS 1 — HTTP APIs        → Business logic, CRUD, queries
  CLASS 2 — WebSocket        → Backend control channel (mute/unmute, timers,
                               session state, mentor commands)
  CLASS 3 — Media (WebRTC)   → Voice/Video — NEVER through API layer
  CLASS 4 — Async Events     → Kafka (analytics, scoring, billing signals)

  RULE: Media plane (CLASS 3) is FULLY ISOLATED from API plane (CLASS 1/2).
        Control plane commands MUST NOT be blocked by video transmission.
        WebSocket channel (CLASS 2) is the ONLY backend→frontend command path
        for GD orchestration — no REST polling for real-time commands.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  A. MEDIA SERVER COMPONENTS (ALL REQUIRED — ABSENCE = STOP EXECUTION)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ COMPONENT 1 — JITSI (VOICE GD ENGINE)                                   │
  │ USE CASE    : Dojo Group Discussion (GD) rooms — voice only             │
  │ TYPE        : Self-hosted, audio-only SFU                               │
  │ DEPLOYMENT  : Kubernetes namespace: media                               │
  │ SUB-COMPONENTS:                                                          │
  │   Jitsi Videobridge  → Server-side audio routing, multi-participant SFU  │
  │   Jicofo            → Participant coordination, conference focus mgmt    │
  │   Prosody (XMPP)    → Signaling backbone for room management            │
  │ RULES:                                                                   │
  │   - Video DISABLED in GD rooms (audio only, by configuration lock)       │
  │   - Forced mute/unmute via Jitsi REST API (backend command only)         │
  │   - Room ID = session_id (deterministic, backend-generated)              │
  │   - Max participants per GD room: enforced by Jitsi config + orchestrator│
  │   - Room creation: backend only, never client-initiated                  │
  │   - JWT authentication required for room join (short-lived tokens)       │
  │   - Self-hosted: no cloud SaaS dependency                                │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ COMPONENT 2 — LIVEKIT (VIDEO + AUDIO ENGINE)                            │
  │ USE CASE    : Dojo Match rooms, Interview rooms, Trainer classrooms,     │
  │               Mock interview rooms, Mentor sessions, Arena sessions      │
  │ TYPE        : Self-hosted SFU (Selective Forwarding Unit)                │
  │ DEPLOYMENT  : Kubernetes namespace: media                               │
  │ FEATURES USED:                                                           │
  │   - Simulcast (adaptive layer selection per participant bandwidth)        │
  │   - Codec: VP8 / VP9 (video), Opus (audio)                              │
  │   - E2E encryption: per-room configurable (mentor sessions)              │
  │   - Participant metadata: role binding in JWT payload                    │
  │   - Room recording: cloud recorder plugin (output to MinIO)              │
  │   - Active speaker detection: feeds speaking-time analytics              │
  │ CLIENT SDK:                                                              │
  │   Flutter: livekit_client (LiveKit Flutter SDK) — required               │
  │   React web: NOT USED for live rooms (React layer forbidden in live room)│
  │ RULES:                                                                   │
  │   - Room ID = match_id / interview_id / session_id (backend-generated)   │
  │   - Token = short-lived JWT signed by Token Service (max 4 hours)        │
  │   - Participant metadata = {user_id, tenant_id, domain_track, role}      │
  │   - Role binding verified on join (Egress hook → Token Verification Svc) │
  │   - Provider swappable (architecture not swappable per Dojo Section B)   │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ COMPONENT 3 — COTURN (NAT TRAVERSAL)                                    │
  │ USE CASE    : TURN / STUN server for all WebRTC connections             │
  │ TYPE        : Self-hosted (coturn)                                       │
  │ DEPLOYMENT  : Kubernetes namespace: media (or dedicated VM for UDP perf) │
  │ PORTS:                                                                   │
  │   STUN: UDP 3478 + TLS 5349                                             │
  │   TURN: UDP 3478, TCP 3478, TLS 5349                                     │
  │   Relay UDP: 49152–65535 (configurable range)                            │
  │ RULES:                                                                   │
  │   - TURN credentials: time-limited, HMAC-generated per session           │
  │   - TURN credential TTL: matches session token TTL (max 4 hours)         │
  │   - No static TURN credentials in any codebase or config map             │
  │   - Bandwidth quota per session enforced at TURN level                   │
  │   - Metrics exported to Prometheus (relay bytes, session count, errors)  │
  │   - Mobile / restricted network fallback: TURN TCP 443 enabled          │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ COMPONENT 4 — RECORDING ENGINE (MinIO + LiveKit Egress)                 │
  │ USE CASE    : Session recording for Dojo, Interviews, Trainer classrooms │
  │ STORAGE     : MinIO (self-hosted S3-compatible object store)             │
  │ TRIGGER     : Only after explicit per-session consent confirmed          │
  │ OUTPUT      : WebM (VP8 + Opus) or MP4 (H.264 + AAC) per config        │
  │ PIPELINE:                                                                │
  │   LiveKit Egress → recording process → MinIO bucket                     │
  │   MinIO event → Kafka topic: media.recording.complete                   │
  │   Kafka → Analytics Service (session analytics trigger)                  │
  │   Kafka → Scoring Engine (post-session scoring trigger)                  │
  │ STORAGE PATH: /tenant_id/session_type/year/month/session_id/             │
  │ RULES:                                                                   │
  │   - Recordings NEVER stored without explicit consent (CM-E enforced)     │
  │   - Consent captured per-session, per-participant (not blanket)          │
  │   - Recording access URL: presigned MinIO URL (TTL = 24h max)            │
  │   - Recording access = logged (DAL-E enforced)                           │
  │   - Replay access = logged separately from recording access              │
  │   - PII overlay in recordings: FORBIDDEN (PM-G enforced)                 │
  │   - Transcripts: generated only with explicit consent; PII redacted       │
  │   - Recording files encrypted at rest: AES-256 (MinIO SSE)              │
  │   - Recording backup: aligned with BR-K (media backups per retention)    │
  │   - Recording deleted on GDPR/DPDP erasure request                       │
  │   - No permanent recordings by default (opt-in only)                     │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  B. KUBERNETES MEDIA NAMESPACE (FIXED LAYOUT)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  NAMESPACE: media
    Deployments:
      jitsi-videobridge       (audio SFU for GD rooms)
      jicofo                  (conference coordinator)
      prosody                 (XMPP signaling)
      livekit-server          (video SFU for match/interview/classroom)
      livekit-egress          (recording controller)
      coturn                  (TURN/STUN server)

  NAMESPACE: realtime
    Deployments:
      voice-gd-orchestrator   (GD state machine + WebSocket command channel)
      dojo-match-engine       (match creation, scenario, role binding)
      interview-service       (scheduling, token gen, interview lifecycle)
      session-token-service   (short-lived media token issuance)

  NAMESPACE RULES:
    - media namespace has NO outbound internet access (isolated)
    - realtime namespace communicates with media namespace via internal DNS
    - No cross-namespace ingress from public internet to media namespace
    - All external traffic to realtime via API Gateway (Traefik)
    - WebSocket gateway: dedicated ingress route (not HTTP API ingress)
    - UDP traffic for WebRTC: LoadBalancer service (not ClusterIP)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
4️⃣  SESSION TYPES & ROOM SPECIFICATIONS (ALL REQUIRED)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ SESSION TYPE            │ ENGINE   │ MODE       │ MAX PAX │ RECORDING │ TURN-BASED │ PROCTOR │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Dojo GD Room            │ Jitsi    │ Audio Only │ 2–10    │ Opt-in    │ YES        │ YES     │
  │ Dojo Match Room         │ LiveKit  │ Audio+Video│ 2–6     │ Opt-in    │ NO         │ YES     │
  │ Interview Room          │ LiveKit  │ Audio+Video│ 2–4     │ Opt-in    │ NO         │ NO      │
  │ Trainer Live Classroom  │ LiveKit  │ Audio+Video│ 2–200   │ Opt-in    │ NO         │ NO      │
  │ Mock Interview Room     │ LiveKit  │ Audio+Video│ 2–4     │ Opt-in    │ NO         │ NO      │
  │ Mentor Session          │ LiveKit  │ Audio+Video│ 2–6     │ Opt-in    │ NO         │ NO      │
  │ Arena Live Session      │ LiveKit  │ Audio+Video│ 2–10    │ Opt-in    │ NO         │ YES     │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ROOM IDENTITY RULES (LOCKED):
    - Room ID = deterministically generated by backend (session_id or match_id)
    - Rooms are NEVER client-created or client-named
    - Room names are OPAQUE (UUID-based, not human-readable externally)
    - One room per active session — no room reuse across sessions
    - Expired rooms: automatically terminated by orchestrator after session TTL

  PARTICIPANT ROLES IN ROOMS (LOCKED):
    - HOST          : Trainer (classroom), Recruiter (interview), Platform admin
    - SPEAKER       : Student (when speaking turn active in GD)
    - LISTENER      : Student (when not speaking turn in GD), evaluator-only joins
    - EVALUATOR     : Evaluator / Mentor (scoring, no turn-based restriction)
    - MODERATOR     : Mentor, Proctor, Admin (can mute/remove, has command priority)
    - PROCTOR       : Assigned proctor in Arena / Dojo match (anti-cheat oversight)
    - OBSERVER      : Parent (read-only, zero media send capability)

  ROLE BINDING RULES:
    - Role embedded in JWT token payload at issuance
    - Role verified by LiveKit / Jitsi on join (hook validation)
    - Role changes mid-session: requires new token reissue + audit log
    - Cross-role privilege escalation mid-session: FORBIDDEN

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
5️⃣  TOKEN SERVICE (MEDIA TOKEN ISSUANCE — LOCKED)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

SERVICE NAME          = Session Token Service (media namespace: realtime)
PROTOCOL              = Internal REST API (not publicly exposed)
CALLER                = Voice GD Orchestrator, Interview Service, Dojo Match Engine,
                        Trainer Session Service, Arena Service ONLY
TOKEN TYPE            = Short-lived signed JWT (LiveKit room token / Jitsi JWT)
TOKEN TTL             = Maximum 4 hours; minimum = session duration estimate + 10 min
SIGNING KEY           = Managed via Hashicorp Vault OSS (auto-rotated every 7 days)
SIGNING ALGORITHM     = HS256 (shared secret) or RS256 (asymmetric — enterprise tier)

TOKEN PAYLOAD SPEC (MANDATORY FIELDS):
  {
    "sub"          : user_id (UUID),
    "iss"          : "ecoskiller-token-service",
    "room"         : room_id (= session_id / match_id — backend assigned),
    "tenant_id"    : UUID (mandatory — non-nullable),
    "domain_track" : ENUM [Arts|Commerce|Science|Technology|Administration],
    "role"         : ENUM [HOST|SPEAKER|LISTENER|EVALUATOR|MODERATOR|PROCTOR|OBSERVER],
    "can_publish"  : Boolean (controlled by role + turn engine state),
    "can_subscribe": Boolean (always true for valid participants),
    "can_record"   : Boolean (only if recording consent confirmed + role=HOST/MODERATOR),
    "metadata"     : { "display_name_masked": String, "belt_tier": String },
    "exp"          : Unix timestamp (TTL enforced),
    "iat"          : Unix timestamp (issued at),
    "jti"          : UUID (token ID — for revocation tracking)
  }

TOKEN RULES (HARD LOCK):
  - Backend issues token ONLY after:
      (1) User authenticated + session authorized (RBAC/ABAC check)
      (2) Consent captured (if recording session)
      (3) Join window validated (GD: batch join window must be open)
  - Token is single-use per room join attempt
  - Token revocation: via jti blocklist in Redis (TTL = token TTL)
  - Expired tokens: rejected at media server level (not retried silently)
  - Token never transmitted via URL parameter (header or WebSocket handshake only)
  - Unsigned tokens: FORBIDDEN → SECURITY_FAILURE
  - TURN credentials: generated alongside token using HMAC-SHA1
    (coturn time-limited credential model) — TTL matches token TTL
  - No static API keys in any client code, config, or environment file

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
6️⃣  VOICE GD ORCHESTRATOR — DOJO ENGINE (CRITICAL MODULE)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

DESCRIPTION:
  The Voice GD Orchestrator is the CRITICAL real-time control brain of the
  Dojo GD engine. It replaces human moderators with a deterministic,
  Redis-backed state machine. It controls every speaking turn, timer,
  mute/unmute command, and session state transition.

  RUNTIME: Node.js (preferred) or Spring Boot
  STATE ENGINE: Redis deterministic state machine (append-only state transitions)
  COMMAND CHANNEL: WebSocket (backend → Flutter client)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  A. GD SESSION LIFECYCLE STATE MACHINE (LOCKED)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  STATES (Canonical — immutable enum):
    GD_CREATED        → Room allocated, tokens not yet issued
    GD_JOIN_WINDOW    → Batch join window open (fixed duration, e.g. 5 min)
    GD_BRIEFING       → All participants joined; scenario/topic announced
    GD_IN_PROGRESS    → Turn-based discussion active
    GD_PAUSE          → System-initiated pause (network issue, admin command)
    GD_CLOSING        → Final remarks phase (all-speak window)
    GD_EVALUATING     → Discussion ended; scoring window open
    GD_COMPLETED      → Scores submitted; session closed; room deallocated
    GD_ABANDONED      → Quorum not met or catastrophic failure; session void

  TRANSITIONS (Enforced by Orchestrator — no client-driven transitions):
    GD_CREATED        → GD_JOIN_WINDOW    (scheduler trigger at batch time)
    GD_JOIN_WINDOW    → GD_BRIEFING       (quorum met: min participants joined)
    GD_JOIN_WINDOW    → GD_ABANDONED      (quorum not met at window close)
    GD_BRIEFING       → GD_IN_PROGRESS    (briefing timer expired)
    GD_IN_PROGRESS    → GD_PAUSE          (admin command / system trigger)
    GD_IN_PROGRESS    → GD_CLOSING        (all turns completed / time limit)
    GD_PAUSE          → GD_IN_PROGRESS    (admin resume command)
    GD_CLOSING        → GD_EVALUATING     (closing timer expired)
    GD_EVALUATING     → GD_COMPLETED      (all evaluations submitted)
    GD_EVALUATING     → GD_COMPLETED      (evaluation timeout — auto-close)
    Any state         → GD_ABANDONED      (admin force-abandon)

  STATE STORAGE (Redis):
    KEY FORMAT: gd:session:{session_id}:state
    VALUE: {
      current_state: ENUM,
      entered_at: Unix timestamp,
      participant_count: Int,
      current_speaker_id: UUID | null,
      turn_index: Int,
      turn_order: [UUID, ...],
      time_remaining_seconds: Int,
      anti_cheat_flags: Int,
      recording_active: Boolean
    }

  RULES:
    - State transitions are ATOMIC (Redis Lua script or WATCH/MULTI/EXEC)
    - No state can be skipped (Custom state machines: FORBIDDEN)
    - Invalid transition attempt = REJECTED + audit log entry
    - State replicated to PostgreSQL on every transition (durable record)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  B. TURN-BASED SPEAKING ENGINE (LOCKED)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ALGORITHM (Deterministic — not randomized in production):
    1. Turn order computed by Orchestrator at GD_BRIEFING state
       (round-robin seeded by session_id — deterministic across runs)
    2. On turn start:
       → Orchestrator sends WebSocket command: UNMUTE {user_id}
       → Jitsi API: participant.setAudioMuted(false) for speaker
       → All other participants: setAudioMuted(true) by Jitsi API
       → Speaking timer started in Redis (countdown key with TTL)
    3. During turn:
       → Speaking time logged in real-time (Orchestrator listens to
         Jitsi audio activity events via REST hook)
       → Speaking time delta written to Redis: gd:session:{id}:speaking:{uid}
    4. On turn end (timer expiry or early hand-off):
       → Orchestrator sends MUTE {user_id} via WebSocket + Jitsi API
       → Turn index incremented
       → Next participant UNMUTED
       → Analytics event emitted: gd.turn.completed
    5. Interruption handling:
       → Participant speaks when not their turn → detected by audio activity
       → Interrupt count incremented: gd:session:{id}:interrupts:{uid}
       → Above threshold: AUTO-MUTE + anti-cheat flag
    6. Turn skipping (participant disconnected):
       → FIFO processing: skip disconnected participant's turn
       → Continue to next in order
       → Reconnect: if within reconnect window (3s), resume turn

  NUMERIC DATA CAPTURE (per turn, per participant):
    - speaking_time_seconds      (from Jitsi audio activity hook)
    - interruption_count         (from audio detection)
    - unmute_attempts_blocked    (attempts while muted)
    - words_estimated            (audio duration proxy — not transcript)
    - turn_completed             (Boolean)
    - turn_skipped_reason        (null | DISCONNECTED | TIMEOUT | SKIPPED)

  All turn data written atomically to Redis + emitted to Kafka:
    Topic: gd.turn.data
    Consumer: Analytics Service + Scoring Engine

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  C. FORCED MUTE / UNMUTE CONTROL (HARD LOCK)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  COMMANDS (Backend → Client only — client CANNOT self-unmute if muted by system):
    SYSTEM_MUTE    : Participant muted by Orchestrator (turn management)
    SYSTEM_UNMUTE  : Participant unmuted for their turn
    MENTOR_MUTE    : Mentor/Moderator manual mute (logged + audited)
    MENTOR_UNMUTE  : Mentor/Moderator manual unmute (logged + audited)
    AUTO_MUTE      : Anti-cheat triggered auto-mute (interrupt threshold breach)
    REMOVE         : Mentor/Admin removes participant from room (audit log required)

  EXECUTION PATH:
    Orchestrator / Mentor command
      → WebSocket command to Flutter client (UI indication)
      → Jitsi REST API: PATCH /colibri/conferences/{room}/participants/{id}
        { "muted": true/false }
      → Jitsi enforces server-side (client cannot override)
      → Audit log entry: {actor, command_type, target_user, session_id, ts}

  RULES:
    - System mute/unmute: ALWAYS server-enforced (not client-side toggle)
    - Client mute toggle: allowed ONLY for self-mute when it is the user's turn
    - Self-mute during someone else's turn: no effect (already system-muted)
    - Mentor commands: require MODERATOR role in JWT
    - All mute/unmute commands: immutably logged (AL-D enforced)
    - Auto-mute for repeated offenders (RL-H: flooding/spam = AUTO-MUTE)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  D. JOIN WINDOW & BATCH MANAGEMENT (LOCKED)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  BATCH CREATION:
    - GD sessions created in configurable batches (e.g. 4–10 participants)
    - Batch composition: domain-track-isolated (Arts batch ≠ Commerce batch)
    - Tenant-isolated batches (cross-tenant batch: FORBIDDEN)
    - Batch created by Orchestrator scheduler (Airflow DAG or cron job)
    - Participants notified N minutes before join window opens (Notification Svc)

  JOIN WINDOW:
    - Window duration: configurable per session type (default: 5 minutes)
    - Window opens at scheduled session time
    - Token issuance: ONLY within join window
    - Late join (after window): FORBIDDEN (token rejected)
    - Quorum threshold: configurable (default: minimum 2 participants)
    - Below quorum at window close: → GD_ABANDONED (participants notified)

  NO-SHOW HANDLING:
    - No-show recorded per participant: gd_no_show event → Kafka
    - Repeated no-shows: flagged for Analytics (churn risk signal)
    - No automatic penalty scoring (human admin review for patterns)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
7️⃣  CONSENT MANAGEMENT — VIDEO_CONNECT ENFORCEMENT (CM-E BINDING)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

CONSENT GATE: Recording CANNOT start without confirmed consent from ALL participants.
CONSENT MODEL: Per-session, per-participant (not account-level blanket consent).

CONSENT CAPTURE FLOW:
  1. Session scheduled → Pre-session notification sent (includes recording notice)
  2. On room join → Flutter UI renders Consent Modal BEFORE audio/video starts:
       "This session will be recorded. Do you consent?"
       [YES, I CONSENT] [NO, JOIN WITHOUT RECORDING]
  3. Consent response captured:
       → POST /consent/capture {session_id, user_id, consent_type: "recording",
                                 response: "granted"|"denied", timestamp}
       → Consent record stored (PostgreSQL: consent_records — immutable)
       → Consent event emitted: Kafka topic: consent.captured
  4. Token Service checks consent status before issuing can_record: true
  5. Recording START:
       → ALL participants must have responded (granted or denied)
       → If ANY participant denies: recording DISABLED for full session
       → If ALL grant: recording enabled; livekit-egress activated
  6. Mid-session consent withdrawal:
       → Participant can withdraw consent via UI button
       → Signal sent to Orchestrator via WebSocket
       → Recording STOPPED within 30 seconds of withdrawal
       → Existing recording segment retained (already captured; legal basis)
       → New segments after withdrawal: NOT recorded
       → Withdrawal event: immutable audit log

DOMAIN-AWARE CONSENT ISOLATION (CM-D):
  - Arts session consent ≠ Commerce or Science session consent
  - Consent record scoped to: session_id + domain_track + tenant_id
  - Domain reuse forbidden

CONSENT STORAGE:
  TABLE: consent_records
    id              UUID PK
    session_id      UUID NOT NULL
    user_id         UUID NOT NULL
    tenant_id       UUID NOT NULL
    domain_track    ENUM NOT NULL
    consent_type    ENUM (recording | transcript | replay_access)
    response        ENUM (granted | denied | withdrawn)
    ip_address      Nullable(String) [masked in display]
    device_id       Nullable(String)
    captured_at     DateTime NOT NULL
    withdrawn_at    Nullable(DateTime)
    is_active       Boolean DEFAULT TRUE
  IMMUTABILITY: No UPDATE or DELETE on consent_records (append-only)

TRANSCRIPT CONSENT (separate gate):
  - Transcript generation requires separate explicit consent
  - Transcript = opt-in beyond recording consent
  - Transcripts stored separately; PII redacted before storage (PM-G)
  - Transcripts deleted when parent recording is deleted

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
8️⃣  ANTI-CHEAT & PROCTORING ENGINE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

SCOPE: Dojo GD rooms, Dojo Match rooms, Arena live sessions.
AUTHORITY: AI generates advisory signals; human Proctor/Moderator acts.
           AI CANNOT auto-ban, auto-score, or terminate rooms without human command.

DETECTION SIGNALS (all real-time, all advisory-labeled):

  SIGNAL-01: SPEAKING_TURN_VIOLATION
    TRIGGER  : Audio activity detected from non-speaking participant
    METHOD   : Jitsi audio activity webhook → Orchestrator
    ACTION   : auto-mute if threshold exceeded; flag emitted to Proctor UI
    THRESHOLD: Configurable per session type (default: 2 violations = flag)

  SIGNAL-02: MULTIPLE_DEVICE_JOIN
    TRIGGER  : Same user_id joining from >1 device in same session
    METHOD   : Token Service detects duplicate active jti for same user+room
    ACTION   : Second join rejected; alert to Proctor; audit log
    EXCEPTION: Reconnect from same device (same jti reuse within 30s grace)

  SIGNAL-03: NETWORK_MANIPULATION
    TRIGGER  : Abnormal disconnect/reconnect pattern (>3 reconnects in 5 min)
    METHOD   : Orchestrator tracks reconnect events per participant
    ACTION   : Advisory flag; above threshold: Proctor notified + auto-pause turn

  SIGNAL-04: AUDIO_INJECTION
    TRIGGER  : AI audio classifier detects non-human audio pattern
               (pre-recorded speech, synthetic voice indicators)
    METHOD   : Optional: audio stream sampling (only if proctoring enabled)
    ACTION   : Advisory flag sent to Proctor UI (human reviews)
    AI LABEL : "AI-Suggested: Possible Audio Injection — Review Required"

  SIGNAL-05: SCORE_GAMING
    TRIGGER  : Peer scores submitted with statistical outlier pattern
               (variance detection — Scoring Engine)
    METHOD   : Variance analysis post-session
    ACTION   : Flags variance_flag in scoring record; mentor review required
    RULE     : Belt promotion blocked if variance_flag = true AND unreviewed

  PROCTOR UI (Flutter — MODERATOR role):
    - Live participant list with real-time flag indicators
    - Anti-cheat event feed (signal type, timestamp, participant)
    - One-click MUTE / REMOVE controls (all actions logged)
    - Flag escalation button (→ Admin Governance Service)
    - Session recording status indicator

  ANTI-CHEAT EVENT STORAGE:
    TABLE: anti_cheat_events
      id              UUID PK
      session_id      UUID NOT NULL
      tenant_id       UUID NOT NULL
      domain_track    ENUM NOT NULL
      participant_id  UUID NOT NULL
      signal_type     ENUM (all signals above)
      ai_confidence   Float32 (0.0–1.0, labeled)
      proctor_action  Nullable(ENUM) (muted|removed|dismissed|escalated)
      reviewed_by     Nullable(UUID) (Proctor user_id)
      reviewed_at     Nullable(DateTime)
      created_at      DateTime NOT NULL
    IMMUTABILITY: No UPDATE or DELETE (append-only)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
9️⃣  QUALITY OF SERVICE (QoS) ENGINE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

PERFORMANCE SLOs (HARD LOCK — from R49.5):
  Room Join Time           ≤ 2 seconds (from token received to in-room)
  Audio Latency            ≤ 150 ms    (end-to-end, measured at SFU)
  Video Latency            ≤ 300 ms    (end-to-end, measured at SFU)
  Mentor Control Signal    ≤ 100 ms    (WebSocket command → execution)
  Reconnect Recovery       ≤ 3 seconds (from drop to active in-room)
  Recording Start Lag      ≤ 5 seconds (from consent confirmed to egress active)

VIOLATION POLICY:
  - SLO breach logged as media_qos_breach event → Kafka
  - Repeated breach (>3 in session): P2 alert → Slack + Grafana annotation
  - Scoring integrity preserved on bandwidth downgrade
    (score not penalized for network-induced speaking interruptions)

QoS METRICS COLLECTED (per participant, per session):
  - audio_jitter_ms             (measured at SFU, per participant stream)
  - audio_packet_loss_pct       (%)
  - video_resolution            (captured)
  - video_bitrate_kbps          (adaptive)
  - round_trip_time_ms          (ICE candidate RTT)
  - reconnect_count             (session total)
  - freeze_duration_seconds     (video freeze events)
  - join_time_ms                (actual join duration)

QoS DATA PIPELINE:
  LiveKit metrics webhook / Jitsi Colibri API
    → Orchestrator / Media QoS Collector
    → Kafka topic: media.qos.events
    → ANALYTICS_CONNECT (AntiGravity) → ClickHouse (dw_fact_media_qos)

ADAPTIVE BITRATE (Simulcast — LiveKit):
  LAYERS:     Low (180p), Mid (360p), High (720p) — auto-selected per receiver BW
  AUDIO-ONLY FALLBACK:
    TRIGGER   : Participant bandwidth < threshold (configurable, default 200 kbps)
    ACTION    : Video layer dropped; audio-only mode activated (UI banner shown)
    SCORING   : Audio-only fallback does NOT affect evaluation score
    RECOVERY  : When bandwidth recovers: video layers restored automatically

NETWORK QUALITY INDICATOR (Flutter UI):
  - 5-bar signal indicator per participant (computed from RTT + jitter + loss)
  - Shown to participant for self-awareness
  - Low quality: yellow warning banner
  - Critical quality: red banner + audio-only mode auto-triggered

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🔟  RECONNECT & RESILIENCE ENGINE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

RECONNECT POLICY (LOCKED):
  DETECTION:
    - Participant WebSocket connection drop → Orchestrator detects within 2s
    - LiveKit participant disconnect event → Orchestrator receives callback
  GRACE WINDOW: 30 seconds (participant reconnect allowed without penalty)
  RECONNECT PROCESS:
    1. Flutter client detects WebSocket/LiveKit drop
    2. Flutter auto-reconnect attempt (exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s)
    3. New token issued automatically (if within grace window):
       → GET /session/reconnect-token {session_id, user_id}
       → Returns new short-lived token (same room, same role)
    4. Flutter re-joins LiveKit/Jitsi room with new token
    5. Role re-binding on reconnect: new token carries original role
    6. UI: reconnecting spinner + "Reconnecting..." banner
    7. If within speaking turn: turn timer paused during grace window
    8. After grace window: turn skipped (FIFO processing resumes)
  POST-RECONNECT:
    - FIFO queue of pending WebSocket commands replayed (ordered)
    - Session state synced from Orchestrator (full state dump on reconnect)
    - Score submission queue replayed if pending (offline queue pattern)

  CATASTROPHIC FAILURE (SFU crash):
    - Jitsi / LiveKit pod crash → Kubernetes auto-restart (readiness probe)
    - Session state preserved in Redis (survives SFU pod restart)
    - All participants reconnect automatically on SFU recovery
    - If SFU down > 60s: session paused (GD_PAUSE state)
    - If SFU down > 300s: session abandoned (GD_ABANDONED) + participants notified
    - Incident logged: P1 alert → PagerDuty

  FLUTTER UX REQUIREMENTS (P7 LOCK):
    - Reconnect UX for live match: in-app reconnecting screen
    - Background resume handling: foreground return triggers reconnect
    - Network drop recovery: auto-reconnect (no manual user action required)
    - Crash recovery screen: graceful UI with "Rejoining session..." state
    - Push notifications: "Your session is waiting" if app backgrounded during GD
    - Queued score submit: pending submissions queued; replayed on reconnect

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1️⃣1️⃣  DATABASE SCHEMA — VIDEO_CONNECT (PostgreSQL)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

TABLE: media_sessions
  PURPOSE: Master record for every video/voice session
  id                UUID           PK
  session_type      ENUM           (gd_room|dojo_match|interview|trainer_classroom|
                                    mock_interview|mentor_session|arena_session)
  tenant_id         UUID           NOT NULL
  domain_track      ENUM           NOT NULL
  room_id           TEXT           NOT NULL UNIQUE (= session_id/match_id)
  media_engine      ENUM           (jitsi|livekit)
  scheduled_at      TIMESTAMP
  join_window_open  TIMESTAMP
  join_window_close TIMESTAMP
  started_at        TIMESTAMP
  ended_at          TIMESTAMP
  state             ENUM           (see GD lifecycle states)
  participant_count INT
  quorum_met        BOOLEAN
  recording_enabled BOOLEAN        DEFAULT FALSE
  recording_path    TEXT           NULL (MinIO path, populated after recording)
  anti_cheat_flags  INT            DEFAULT 0
  created_at        TIMESTAMP
  updated_at        TIMESTAMP

TABLE: session_participants
  PURPOSE: Participant roster per session
  id                UUID           PK
  session_id        UUID           FK → media_sessions
  user_id           UUID           NOT NULL
  tenant_id         UUID           NOT NULL
  role              ENUM           (HOST|SPEAKER|LISTENER|EVALUATOR|
                                    MODERATOR|PROCTOR|OBSERVER)
  joined_at         TIMESTAMP
  left_at           TIMESTAMP
  reconnect_count   INT            DEFAULT 0
  no_show           BOOLEAN        DEFAULT FALSE
  speaking_time_sec INT            DEFAULT 0
  turn_count        INT            DEFAULT 0
  interruption_count INT           DEFAULT 0
  token_jti         UUID           (for revocation tracking)

TABLE: session_turn_log
  PURPOSE: Immutable per-turn record (GD only)
  id                UUID           PK
  session_id        UUID           FK → media_sessions
  participant_id    UUID           FK → session_participants
  turn_index        INT
  started_at        TIMESTAMP
  ended_at          TIMESTAMP
  speaking_time_sec INT
  turn_status       ENUM           (completed|skipped|interrupted|system_ended)
  skip_reason       ENUM NULL      (disconnected|timeout|no_audio)

TABLE: media_audit_log
  PURPOSE: Immutable audit log for all media events (AL-D enforced)
  id                UUID           PK
  session_id        UUID           NOT NULL
  tenant_id         UUID           NOT NULL
  domain_track      ENUM           NOT NULL
  actor_id          UUID           NULL (system events: null)
  actor_type        ENUM           (user|system|orchestrator|proctor|admin)
  event_type        ENUM           (room_created|room_deleted|participant_join|
                                    participant_leave|role_change|recording_start|
                                    recording_stop|mute_command|unmute_command|
                                    remove_command|consent_captured|consent_withdrawn|
                                    anti_cheat_flag|turn_start|turn_end|
                                    session_state_change|reconnect|token_revoked)
  target_user_id    UUID           NULL
  event_detail      JSONB          (event-specific payload, no PII)
  timestamp         TIMESTAMP      NOT NULL
  IMMUTABILITY: No UPDATE or DELETE. Append-only enforced at DB level.

TABLE: recording_index
  PURPOSE: Index of all session recordings
  id                UUID           PK
  session_id        UUID           FK → media_sessions
  tenant_id         UUID           NOT NULL
  domain_track      ENUM           NOT NULL
  minio_path        TEXT           NOT NULL
  format            ENUM           (webm|mp4)
  duration_seconds  INT
  file_size_bytes   BIGINT
  sha256_checksum   TEXT           NOT NULL (integrity verification)
  transcript_path   TEXT           NULL (MinIO path if transcript exists)
  recording_status  ENUM           (processing|ready|failed|deleted)
  created_at        TIMESTAMP
  deleted_at        TIMESTAMP      NULL (soft delete — erasure flow)
  legal_hold        BOOLEAN        DEFAULT FALSE

TABLE: consent_records
  [Defined in §7 — do not re-declare schema here]

TABLE: anti_cheat_events
  [Defined in §8 — do not re-declare schema here]

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1️⃣2️⃣  KAFKA EVENT CONTRACTS (VIDEO_CONNECT → PLATFORM)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

ALL EVENTS FOLLOW THE PLATFORM EVENT ENVELOPE (from ANALYTICS_CONNECT §3).
Additional VIDEO_CONNECT-specific event topics:

  TOPIC: media.session.created
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, session_type, tenant_id, domain_track,
               room_id, media_engine, scheduled_at }
    CONSUMERS: Analytics Service, Notification Service

  TOPIC: media.session.state_changed
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, tenant_id, domain_track,
               from_state, to_state, timestamp }
    CONSUMERS: Analytics Service, Scoring Engine, Dojo Match Engine

  TOPIC: media.participant.joined
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, user_id, tenant_id, domain_track, role, joined_at }
    CONSUMERS: Analytics Service, Anti-Cheat Monitor, Notification Service

  TOPIC: media.participant.left
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, user_id, tenant_id, domain_track,
               left_at, speaking_time_sec, reconnect_count }
    CONSUMERS: Analytics Service, Scoring Engine

  TOPIC: media.turn.completed
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, participant_id, tenant_id, domain_track,
               turn_index, speaking_time_sec, turn_status }
    CONSUMERS: Analytics Service, Scoring Engine

  TOPIC: media.recording.started
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, tenant_id, domain_track, recording_id,
               consent_all_granted: true }
    CONSUMERS: Analytics Service, Audit Service

  TOPIC: media.recording.completed
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, recording_id, tenant_id, minio_path,
               duration_seconds, sha256_checksum }
    CONSUMERS: Analytics Service, Scoring Engine, Notification Service

  TOPIC: media.anti_cheat.flagged
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, participant_id, tenant_id, domain_track,
               signal_type, ai_confidence }
    CONSUMERS: Proctor Service, Analytics Service, Admin Governance

  TOPIC: media.qos.events
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, participant_id, tenant_id, domain_track,
               metric_name, metric_value, timestamp }
    CONSUMERS: ANALYTICS_CONNECT (AntiGravity)

  TOPIC: gd.completed
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, tenant_id, domain_track,
               participant_summaries: [{user_id, speaking_time_sec,
               turn_count, anti_cheat_flags}] }
    CONSUMERS: Scoring Engine, Belt Engine, Analytics Service

  TOPIC: interview.completed
    PAYLOAD: { session_id, job_id, applicant_id, recruiter_id,
               tenant_id, domain_track, duration_seconds }
    CONSUMERS: Interview Service, Analytics Service, Notification Service

EVENT RULES:
  - All events follow Avro schema (registered in schema registry)
  - All events include tenant_id (non-nullable)
  - Deduplication: source_event_id prevents replay from double-processing
  - No PII in event payloads (user_id reference only)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1️⃣3️⃣  INTEGRATION WIRING — CROSS-ENGINE (P6 LOCK — MANDATORY)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

ALL WIRING IS EVENT-DRIVEN. NO MANUAL SYNC ALLOWED.

  MATCH ENGINE ↔ VIDEO ROOM LIFECYCLE:
    Dojo Match Engine creates match → emits match.created
    Voice GD Orchestrator consumes → creates room → issues tokens
    Match end → emits match.ended → Orchestrator tears down room

  MATCH END ↔ RECORDING FINALIZE:
    media.session.state_changed (→ GD_EVALUATING)
    → livekit-egress stop recording
    → Recording uploaded to MinIO
    → media.recording.completed emitted

  RECORDING FINALIZE ↔ ANALYTICS TRIGGER:
    media.recording.completed consumed by Analytics Service
    → Session analytics job triggered (speaking time, QoS summary)
    → Results written to ClickHouse (dw_fact_dojo_session,
      dw_fact_dojo_participant_score)

  ANALYTICS RESULT ↔ BELT ELIGIBILITY CHECK:
    gd.completed consumed by Belt Engine
    → Eligibility check: match_count, score_threshold, pressure_pass
    → If eligible: mentor confirmation requested (not auto-promoted)
    → Belt.eligible event emitted → Notification Service → Mentor

  MENTOR OVERRIDE ↔ RATING RECOMPUTE:
    Mentor submits score override (MODERATOR role required)
    → Scoring Engine recomputes merged score
    → Variance detection re-run
    → Recomputed score stored (immutable new record, not update)
    → Audit log entry (mentor_id, override_reason, original_score, new_score)

  CERTIFICATION ↔ CERTIFICATE GENERATOR:
    Belt Engine confirms promotion → certification.awarded emitted
    → Certificate Generator (PDF) → stored in MinIO
    → Notification Service → User + Institute (if applicable)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1️⃣4️⃣  FLUTTER CLIENT IMPLEMENTATION (VIDEO_CONNECT LAYER)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

CLIENT STACK (LOCKED):
  Framework     = Flutter (stable channel)
  State Mgmt    = Riverpod
  WebRTC Plugin = livekit_client (LiveKit Flutter SDK) — mandatory
  WebSocket     = dart:io WebSocket (custom command channel client)
  Permissions   = permission_handler (microphone, camera, notifications)

REQUIRED FLUTTER SCREENS:

  SCREEN-01: ConsentModal
    TRIGGER : Before room join (recording sessions)
    ELEMENTS:
      - Session name, session type indicator
      - Clear consent statement (legal-reviewed text)
      - [CONSENT & JOIN] button
      - [JOIN WITHOUT RECORDING] button (if non-recording option available)
      - Consent withdrawal instructions
    RULES   : Cannot be dismissed without selection (blocking modal)
              Must render before any audio/video device access

  SCREEN-02: LiveGDRoom (Jitsi — Audio Only)
    ELEMENTS:
      - Participant list with role badge + speaking indicator ring
      - Turn indicator: "NOW SPEAKING: [Username]" (animated)
      - Timer bar (countdown for current turn)
      - Self-mute indicator (system-muted vs self-muted visual distinction)
      - Anti-cheat warning banner (if auto-mute triggered)
      - Network quality indicator (5-bar signal)
      - [Reconnecting...] overlay (on disconnect)
      - Session state banner (BRIEFING / IN_PROGRESS / CLOSING / EVALUATING)
      - Proctor overlay (MODERATOR role only): participant flags + controls

  SCREEN-03: LiveMatchRoom (LiveKit — Audio + Video)
    ELEMENTS:
      - Video tiles (participant grid, active speaker highlighted)
      - Self camera preview (corner)
      - Audio/video toggle (within user's allowed state)
      - Network quality indicator
      - Session timer
      - Chat panel (rate-limited, RL-H enforced)
      - Screen share button (if MODERATOR/HOST role)
      - [Reconnecting...] overlay
      - Proctor controls (PROCTOR role only)

  SCREEN-04: InterviewRoom (LiveKit)
    ELEMENTS:
      - Two-party video tiles (candidate + recruiter)
      - Timer (interview duration)
      - Network quality indicator
      - [End interview] button (HOST/recruiter only)
      - Post-session satisfaction rating prompt (on end)

  SCREEN-05: TrainerClassroom (LiveKit — up to 200 participants)
    ELEMENTS:
      - Trainer video tile (prominent)
      - Participant roster (count + names)
      - Chat panel
      - Raise hand feature
      - Screen share (HOST role)
      - Attendance counter
      - Recording status indicator (live recording dot if active)
      - Download recording link (post-session, if consented)

  SCREEN-06: RecordingLibrary
    ELEMENTS:
      - List of accessible recordings (per user's consent + role)
      - Playback controls
      - Download button (if role permits)
      - Access log notice: "This access is recorded"
      - Delete request button (GDPR/DPDP right-to-erasure)

  SCREEN-07: RecordingConsentWithdrawal
    TRIGGER : In-session UI button for active recording sessions
    ELEMENTS:
      - Confirmation dialog
      - Effect explanation: "Recording will stop. Already recorded content
        remains per platform policy."
      - [WITHDRAW CONSENT] / [CANCEL] buttons

  RULES FOR ALL SCREENS:
    - Sensitive screens MUST disable screenshots and screen recording
      (Flutter: FLAG_SECURE on Android / SecureView on iOS)
    - No PII overlay on any participant video stream (PM-G)
    - DECORATIVE_UI = FORBIDDEN (inherited from platform UI rules)
    - React web layer MUST NOT render any live room screen

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1️⃣5️⃣  API CONTRACTS (VIDEO_CONNECT SERVICE ENDPOINTS)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

ALL APIs: JWT-authenticated, RBAC-gated, rate-limited (RL-G enforced).
ALL APIs: Internal VPC only (no public exposure for media token endpoints).

  POST /media/session/create
    CALLER  : Orchestrator / Scheduler (internal, not user-facing)
    BODY    : { session_type, tenant_id, domain_track, scheduled_at,
               participant_ids, match_id | interview_id }
    RETURNS : { session_id, room_id, join_window_open, join_window_close }

  POST /media/token/issue
    CALLER  : Flutter client (authenticated user)
    AUTH    : JWT required, within join window
    BODY    : { session_id }
    RETURNS : { livekit_token | jitsi_jwt, turn_credentials,
               room_id, role, token_expiry }
    RULES   : Consent checked before token issued for recording sessions
              TURN credentials included in same response

  POST /media/token/reconnect
    CALLER  : Flutter client (on reconnect)
    AUTH    : JWT required, within grace window (30s)
    BODY    : { session_id, original_jti }
    RETURNS : { new_token, turn_credentials }

  POST /consent/capture
    CALLER  : Flutter client (user action)
    BODY    : { session_id, consent_type, response }
    RETURNS : { consent_id, captured_at, status }

  POST /consent/withdraw
    CALLER  : Flutter client (user action, in-session)
    BODY    : { session_id, consent_type }
    RETURNS : { withdrawn_at, recording_stop_eta_seconds }

  GET  /media/session/{session_id}/state
    CALLER  : Flutter client (for state sync on reconnect)
    RETURNS : { current_state, turn_order, current_speaker_id,
               time_remaining_sec, recording_active }

  POST /media/command/mute
    CALLER  : Orchestrator (system), Mentor/Proctor (MODERATOR role)
    BODY    : { session_id, target_user_id, command_type, reason }
    RETURNS : { command_id, executed_at }
    AUDIT   : Immutable log entry on every call

  GET  /recording/{recording_id}/access
    CALLER  : Authenticated user (RBAC: allowed roles only)
    RETURNS : { presigned_url (TTL: 2 hours), expires_at }
    AUDIT   : Access log entry: DAL-E enforced

  DELETE /recording/{recording_id}
    CALLER  : Data subject (own data) or Compliance Admin
    AUTH    : GDPR/DPDP erasure workflow (multi-step, consent-aware)
    RETURNS : { deletion_scheduled_at, legal_hold: Boolean }

  API SLO (R49.3 — inherited):
    /media/token/issue     ≤ 250 ms P95
    /media/session/state   ≤ 150 ms P95
    /media/command/mute    ≤ 100 ms P95 (mentor commands — Class-1 Interactive)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1️⃣6️⃣  SECURITY HARDENING (P1 SECURITY LOCK — MEDIA-SPECIFIC)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

MEDIA SECURITY CONTROLS (ALL REQUIRED):

  SIGNED VIDEO ROOM TOKENS:
    - Every room join requires signed JWT (see §5 Token Service)
    - Tokens verified by LiveKit / Jitsi on join (hook validation)
    - Unsigned tokens: REJECTED → SECURITY_FAILURE

  SIGNED RECORDING URLS:
    - Recording files served ONLY via MinIO presigned URL
    - TTL: maximum 2 hours per presigned URL
    - URL generated fresh on each /recording/{id}/access call
    - No permanent public URLs for recordings

  TIME-LIMITED REPLAY ACCESS:
    - Replay access authenticated (no anonymous replay)
    - Role-based replay permission (RBAC enforced)
    - Replay access logged (DAL-E: separate log entry per replay)

  ENCRYPTED MEDIA PLANE:
    - SRTP encryption for all WebRTC audio/video streams (mandatory)
    - DTLS-SRTP handshake enforced (no unencrypted fallback)
    - LiveKit room-level encryption: enabled for mentor sessions (E2E option)

  TURN CREDENTIAL SECURITY:
    - HMAC-SHA1 time-limited credentials (coturn TURN REST API model)
    - No static TURN passwords in any config, secret, or code
    - Credentials expire at token TTL (≤ 4 hours)
    - Separate TURN credentials per session (not shared)

  NETWORK ISOLATION:
    - Jitsi / LiveKit ports: NOT exposed via HTTP ingress
    - WebRTC UDP: exposed via dedicated LoadBalancer (not HTTP proxy)
    - TURN: exposed on UDP 3478 / TLS 5349 (dedicated service)
    - Media namespace: no egress to internet (except STUN/TURN relay path)
    - All inter-service: mTLS

  SCREEN & DEVICE SECURITY:
    - Sensitive live room screens: FLAG_SECURE (Android) / SecureView (iOS)
    - Screenshot + screen recording disabled on live room screens
    - Camera/microphone permission: requested only when entering room
    - Permission denied: graceful degradation (audio-only or observer mode)

  RATE LIMITING (RL-H — Dojo real-time):
    - Message send rate: 1 message/500ms per participant
    - Reaction/chat: 5 reactions/10s per participant
    - Join/leave events: throttled (prevent rapid join/leave abuse)
    - Recording access: 10 requests/hour per user
    - Flooding → AUTO-MUTE + TEMP BLOCK

  SECRETS MANAGEMENT:
    - LiveKit API key/secret: Hashicorp Vault OSS (auto-rotated 7-day cycle)
    - Jitsi JWT secret: Hashicorp Vault OSS
    - coturn HMAC secret: Hashicorp Vault OSS
    - MinIO recording bucket credentials: Vault OSS
    - No plaintext secrets in environment files or config maps in production

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1️⃣7️⃣  OBSERVABILITY & MEDIA MONITORING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

METRICS (Prometheus — all media components):

  Jitsi:
    - jitsi_videobridge_conferences_total
    - jitsi_videobridge_participants_total
    - jitsi_videobridge_endpoints_with_high_outbound_loss_total
    - jitsi_videobridge_bit_rate_download / _upload (kbps)

  LiveKit:
    - livekit_room_count
    - livekit_participant_count
    - livekit_publish_track_count
    - livekit_room_join_duration_ms (histogram)
    - livekit_egress_active_count
    - livekit_egress_failed_total

  coturn:
    - coturn_sessions_total
    - coturn_bytes_relayed_total
    - coturn_errors_total
    - coturn_udp_relay_ports_active

  Voice GD Orchestrator:
    - gd_sessions_active
    - gd_sessions_completed_total
    - gd_sessions_abandoned_total
    - gd_turn_duration_seconds (histogram)
    - gd_reconnect_events_total
    - gd_anti_cheat_flags_total
    - gd_mute_commands_total

  Interview Service:
    - interviews_scheduled_total
    - interviews_completed_total
    - interviews_no_show_total
    - interview_satisfaction_avg (post-session)

ALERTING THRESHOLDS:
  P1 (PagerDuty immediate):
    - Jitsi/LiveKit pod crash (health check failure > 30s)
    - GD session fail to start (missed batch start)
    - Recording egress failure (consecutive)
    - TURN server unreachable > 60s
    - SFU down causing GD_ABANDONED for batch

  P2 (Slack + Grafana annotation):
    - Room join time P95 > 4 seconds (double SLO)
    - Audio packet loss > 5% sustained 2 minutes
    - Reconnect rate > 10% of participants in any session
    - Recording file not uploaded within 10 min of session end
    - Anti-cheat flags > 20% of participants in a session

REQUIRED GRAFANA DASHBOARDS:
  - GD Session Health Dashboard (active sessions, state distribution, failures)
  - Interview Room Dashboard (active rooms, no-shows, satisfaction)
  - Media QoS Dashboard (jitter, latency, packet loss, reconnect rate)
  - Coturn Relay Dashboard (sessions, bytes, error rate)
  - Recording Pipeline Dashboard (egress active, processing lag, failure rate)
  - Anti-Cheat Monitor Dashboard (flags by signal type, by session, by domain)

TRACING:
  - OpenTelemetry spans: token issuance → room join → session lifecycle → teardown
  - Jaeger trace sampling: 10% for normal sessions; 100% for error traces
  - Trace IDs correlate: HTTP request → WebSocket event → Kafka event

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1️⃣8️⃣  TESTING REQUIREMENTS (P4 QA LOCK — MEDIA-SPECIFIC)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

ALL TESTS MUST PASS BEFORE PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT CLAIM.

  REALTIME TESTS (required):
    - WebSocket event ordering tests (command delivery order preserved)
    - Reconnect tests (drop + rejoin within 3s SLO)
    - Duplicate event handling (idempotent command processing)
    - FIFO queue replay on reconnect

  VIDEO TESTS (required):
    - 2–10 participant rooms (Jitsi GD + LiveKit match)
    - Join/leave mid-session (turn engine continues correctly)
    - Bandwidth downgrade tests (simulcast layer selection)
    - Audio-only fallback trigger and recovery
    - Recording start/stop (consent gate enforcement)
    - Token expiry during live session (graceful handling)

  GD ORCHESTRATOR TESTS (required):
    - Full state machine traversal (all state transitions)
    - Turn rotation correctness (round-robin validation)
    - Forced mute/unmute execution (Jitsi API mock)
    - Anti-cheat signal detection (audio interrupt threshold)
    - Quorum failure → GD_ABANDONED flow
    - Batch creation correctness (domain + tenant isolation)

  LOAD TESTS (required — R49.12):
    - 10× students joining concurrently (spike join)
    - Reconnect storm simulation (all participants drop + rejoin within 5s)
    - Concurrent room simulation (50 simultaneous GD rooms per tenant)
    - Scoring concurrency (100 simultaneous score submissions)

  SECURITY TESTS (required):
    - Unsigned token rejected
    - Expired token rejected
    - Cross-tenant room join rejected
    - Self-unmute when system-muted: blocked
    - Recording without consent: blocked
    - TURN credential expiry enforcement

  TEST COVERAGE THRESHOLD:
    - Unit test coverage: ≥ 80% (Orchestrator, Token Service, Scoring Engine)
    - Integration tests: all critical wiring paths (§13)
    - No production deploy without passing test gates

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1️⃣9️⃣  LEGAL & COMPLIANCE (P9 LOCK — MEDIA-SPECIFIC)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

MANDATORY DOCUMENTS (must exist before any recording session goes live):
  - Recording Consent Terms (legal-reviewed, displayed at consent modal)
  - Performance Assessment Disclosure (scoring methodology transparency)
  - Mentor Conduct Terms (moderator command rights and limits)
  - Student Data Use Disclosure (speaking time, scores, anti-cheat data)
  - Platform Privacy Policy (covering media data)

JURISDICTION READINESS:
  GDPR (EU):
    - Lawful basis for recording: explicit consent (Art. 6(1)(a))
    - Right to erasure: recording delete workflow (§11 recording_index.deleted_at)
    - Data portability: recording export via presigned URL
    - DPA (Data Processing Agreement) template for enterprise tenants

  India DPDP (Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023):
    - Consent notice in simple language (CM-F requirement)
    - Data Principal rights: access, correction, erasure, nomination
    - Purpose limitation: recording data used only for session + scoring
    - Processing restricted to declared purpose (CM-B)

  MINOR PROTECTION:
    - Parental consent captured (OBSERVER role parent flow)
    - Minor participant flag on consent_records
    - Additional masking level for minor PII in recordings (PM-G + PM-C)

  DATA DELETION WORKFLOW (recording erasure):
    1. User submits erasure request (in-app: Settings → Privacy → My Data)
    2. Request logged: data_deletion_requests table
    3. Recording files in MinIO: soft-deleted (recording_index.deleted_at set)
    4. Legal hold check: if legal_hold = TRUE, erasure blocked (user notified)
    5. After legal hold cleared: hard delete from MinIO
    6. Transcript deleted with parent recording
    7. consent_records: cannot be deleted (legal evidence of consent)
    8. Audit log entry: data_deletion event (immutable)
    9. Confirmation sent to user (within 30 days — DPDP / GDPR deadline)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
2️⃣0️⃣  INFRASTRUCTURE SIZING (R25 — STARTUP STAGE DEFAULTS)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

STARTUP-STAGE MINIMAL VIABLE DEFAULTS:

  Jitsi Videobridge:
    - 1 node | 8 vCPU / 16GB RAM | 1Gbps NIC (audio SFU, low CPU with audio-only)
    - Scale: add nodes horizontally as GD session count grows
    - Target: 50 concurrent GD rooms (audio-only) per node

  LiveKit Server:
    - 1 node | 8 vCPU / 32GB RAM | 1Gbps NIC
    - Scale: horizontal pod scaling (HPA on active room count)
    - Target: 20 concurrent video rooms per node (mixed audio+video)

  LiveKit Egress:
    - 1 node | 4 vCPU / 8GB RAM (CPU-intensive for recording)
    - Scale: 1 egress pod per 5 concurrent recordings
    - Max concurrent recordings: configurable (default: 10 at startup)

  coturn:
    - 1 dedicated VM | 4 vCPU / 8GB RAM | 1Gbps NIC | Public IP required
    - TURN relay bandwidth: 100 Mbps sustained (scale NIC on demand)
    - Scale: add coturn instances behind DNS round-robin

  Voice GD Orchestrator:
    - 1–2 pods | 2 vCPU / 4GB RAM | HPA on concurrent GD sessions
    - Redis: same cluster as platform Redis (separate keyspace prefix)

  Interview Service:
    - 1–2 pods | 2 vCPU / 4GB RAM

  Session Token Service:
    - 2 pods (HA required — token issuance is on critical path)
    - 1 vCPU / 2GB RAM per pod

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
2️⃣1️⃣  DEVELOPMENT BUILD ORDER (SEQUENTIAL — VIDEO_CONNECT SPECIFIC)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

PREREQUISITES (must be complete before VIDEO_CONNECT build):
  ✅ STAGE 1: Auth Service, User Service, Tenant/Domain model live
  ✅ Consent Management Service operational
  ✅ Kafka event bus live + schema registry operational
  ✅ MinIO object store operational
  ✅ PostgreSQL with media_sessions schema deployed

VIDEO_CONNECT BUILD ORDER (Sequential — skipping = INVALID BUILD):

  STEP 01 : coturn deployment + HMAC credential generation verification
  STEP 02 : Jitsi Videobridge + Jicofo + Prosody (Kubernetes media namespace)
            → Audio GD room smoke test (2 participants, mute/unmute)
  STEP 03 : LiveKit Server deployment (Kubernetes media namespace)
            → Video room smoke test (2 participants)
  STEP 04 : Session Token Service (Vault integration, JWT signing)
            → Token issuance + Jitsi/LiveKit join test
  STEP 05 : PostgreSQL schema migrations (Flyway):
            media_sessions, session_participants, session_turn_log,
            media_audit_log, recording_index, consent_records,
            anti_cheat_events
  STEP 06 : Voice GD Orchestrator — Redis state machine
            → State machine unit tests (all transitions)
  STEP 07 : Voice GD Orchestrator — Turn-based speaking engine
            → Turn rotation test, mute/unmute Jitsi API integration
  STEP 08 : Voice GD Orchestrator — WebSocket command channel (Flutter SDK)
  STEP 09 : Consent Management integration (CM-E enforcement at token gate)
  STEP 10 : LiveKit Egress (recording controller) + MinIO integration
            → Recording consent gate enforcement test
            → Recording upload + SHA-256 checksum verification
  STEP 11 : Kafka event producers (all topics per §12)
  STEP 12 : Anti-Cheat Engine (signal detection, Proctor WebSocket alerts)
  STEP 13 : QoS Metrics Collector (LiveKit webhook + Jitsi Colibri API)
            → Prometheus export, Grafana dashboards
  STEP 14 : Flutter screens (ConsentModal, LiveGDRoom, LiveMatchRoom,
            InterviewRoom, TrainerClassroom, RecordingLibrary)
  STEP 15 : Reconnect engine (token refresh + FIFO queue replay)
  STEP 16 : Screen security (FLAG_SECURE Android, SecureView iOS)
  STEP 17 : Integration wiring (§13 — all cross-engine event hooks)
  STEP 18 : Security tests (unsigned token, expired token, cross-tenant)
  STEP 19 : Load tests (R49.12: 10× students, reconnect storms)
  STEP 20 : Legal document integration (consent modal text, privacy policy)
  STEP 21 : Production readiness checklist sign-off
            (CM-O ✅ AL-D ✅ DAL-E ✅ PM-G ✅ RL-H ✅ R49.5 ✅)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🔒  SEAL DECLARATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

MODULE                   : VIDEO_CONNECT (ANTIGRAVITY — MEDIA LAYER)
PARENT MODULE            : ANALYTICS_CONNECT (ANTIGRAVITY)
PARENT SYSTEM            : ECOSKILLER ENTERPRISE MULTI-TENANT SAAS
SEALED BY                : PLATFORM ARCHITECTURE AUTHORITY
MUTATION AUTHORITY       : PLATFORM_ADMIN_ONLY + ADD_ONLY POLICY
OVERRIDE AUTHORITY       : NONE — no creative interpretation permitted

COMPLIANCE SEALS:
  → RBAC + ABAC              ✅
  → MFA + Auth               ✅
  → Tenant Isolation         ✅ (room namespacing + token tenant_id binding)
  → Domain Isolation         ✅ (batch isolation, consent isolation)
  → Consent Management CM-*  ✅ (per-session, per-participant, per-purpose)
  → Audit Logging AL-D       ✅ (all media events, mute commands, recordings)
  → Data Access Logging DAL-E✅ (recording access + replay access logged)
  → PII Masking PM-G         ✅ (no PII overlay in recordings, transcripts)
  → Rate Limiting RL-H       ✅ (Dojo real-time throttling, auto-mute)
  → Data Integrity DI-H      ✅ (recording metadata immutable, scores locked)
  → Backup BR-K              ✅ (recording backups, opt-in only)
  → Media SLOs R49.5         ✅ (join ≤2s, audio ≤150ms, reconnect ≤3s)
  → GDPR + India DPDP        ✅ (erasure workflow, consent capture)
  → Security P1              ✅ (signed tokens, SRTP, TURN credentials)

AI POLICY                : ADVISORY ONLY — Anti-cheat AI flags advisory;
                           human Proctor acts. AI never auto-bans or terminates.
REACT IN LIVE ROOM       : FORBIDDEN (Flutter only for all live rooms)
MEDIA THROUGH API        : FORBIDDEN (media plane strictly isolated)
RECORDING W/O CONSENT    : FORBIDDEN → BLOCK EXECUTION
ANONYMOUS PARTICIPANTS   : FORBIDDEN
CROSS-TENANT ROOMS       : FORBIDDEN → SECURITY_FAILURE
TRANSPORT MODEL CHANGE   : REQUIRES VERSION BUMP
STAGE DEPENDENCY         : STAGE 1 COMPLETE + ANALYTICS_CONNECT LIVE

🔐 THIS MODULE IS SEALED AND LOCKED.
   ALL ADDITIONS MUST FOLLOW ADD_ONLY MUTATION POLICY.
   MODIFICATIONS REQUIRE PLATFORM_ADMIN AUTHORITY + FULL AUDIT LOG ENTRY.
   ANY DEVIATION FROM THIS SPECIFICATION = INVALID BUILD → STOP EXECUTION.

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  🔒 VIDEO_CONNECT — ANTIGRAVITY MEDIA LAYER — SEALED & LOCKED ✅                          ║
